title: Mere Naina Saawan Bhadon

lines.lyrics: Dard bhara yeh geet kahaan se

lines.lyrics: Ab sochoon tumhein yaad nahi hai
Rut aaye rut jaaye de kar
Bijuri ban kar gagan pe chamki

description: This iconic song is a poignant masterpiece, beautifully capturing the agony of separation and longing through its melancholic melody. R.D. Burman masterfully infuses Raag Shivranjani's characteristic pathos, creating a timeless classic that resonates deeply with listeners. Kishore Kumar's soulful rendition adds layers of emotional depth, making it one of his most memorable performances.
trivia: The song exists in two equally famous versions, one sung by Kishore Kumar and the other by Lata Mangeshkar, both featuring the same composition but with distinct emotional nuances.
It is a prime example of R.D. Burman's genius in blending classical Indian ragas with popular film music, making complex melodies accessible and appealing to a wide audience.
Raag Shivranjani, known for its five-note scale (pentatonic) and inherent sadness, is the backbone of this composition, lending it its profound emotional quality.
The lyrics by Anand Bakshi vividly describe the monsoon season (Saawan Bhadon) as a metaphor for tears and sorrow, perfectly complementing the song's melancholic mood.
